I do remember the explanation but I still don't remember fully of the gramona scene, just that she was there.

It’s not shown how she does it but she can slowly drain and steal emotions from people! Doesn’t have to be a show all the time but it’s a good distraction so they don’t notice! Awakeners are harder to steal emotion from and she needs to do it a lot for it to have any notable effect on them. If she steals all of someone’s emotions they become a mindless puppet (her words not mine) 
She steals emotions to try and understand them since she can’t truly feel any herself, although I can’t deny a lot of the time she might just be doing it cause she can <:cute1:1356053053568192717>
We have given every detail of this collaboration the same care we give to our own characters and stories within Morimens.

Hey lore chatters
My assignment led me to making a Morimens critical analysis, I need a lot of help with the inspirations of how H.P Lovecraft inspired the characters
All the help will do
it's not just lovecraft or his extended mythos purely. most characters are also inspired from various non-mythos elements
for example, sanga is based on Camille Claudel, the real life french sculptor. the CG in her deepdive are also a reference to one of her works, The Waltz
you could analyse how biav entwines lovecraftian concepts with non lovecraftian ones, as well as intentional choices made in the design to avoid pitfalls of their authors
why is horla like
semi invisible
why is some of her body gone
part of her abilities
awakener bodies are not necessarily made of flesh. horla's by her own admission says her body is mostly ink and paper
(also a reference to her source material where she was invisible)
i should read the horla at some point
